message:
feat: expose streaming health in IPC status and add resize fallback determinism tests

Three related changes ensuring resize correctness is observable and
testable across the terminal stack:

ipc.rs — streaming health in status payload:
- Add streaming_health field to the IPC status response by calling
  tailer::StreamingHealth::get_global() and serializing the snapshot
- Exposes events_processed, dirty_ranges_total, dirty_rows_total,
  gaps_emitted, fallback_count, and active_panes for external
  monitoring tools
- Update test to populate StreamingHealth global state and verify the
  fields appear correctly in the status JSON response

localpane.rs — resize replay fallback path determinism test:
- New test: replay_fallback_paths_preserve_identical_presented_outcome
  verifies that two different resize conflict resolution strategies
  (boundary cancellation vs present-barrier rejection) produce
  identical final presented geometry, sequence numbers, and completion
  state — ensuring users see the same result regardless of which
  internal fallback path was taken
- Import reordering (rustfmt)

screen.rs — forced rollback retry convergence test:
- New test: forced_rollback_retry_matches_direct_resize_semantics
  verifies that a screen that undergoes a forced rollback (via
  force_resize_commit_rollback) and then retries the resize produces
  identical visible lines, cursor position, and geometry as a screen
  that resized directly without any rollback — proving the rollback
  mechanism is semantically transparent
- Collapse phys_intersection if/else to single-line form (rustfmt)
- Reformat multi-line assert! for readability (rustfmt)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
